summary: Scorching Playin'→Dark Star→Morning Dew suite with tight bandwork
blurb: The Fox Theatre show delivers a high‑energy second‑set marathon highlighted by the iconic Playin’ → Dark Star → Morning Dew sequence and a soaring Bird Song. Jerry’s lead work shines, Phil’s bass is telepathic, and Keith provides solid keys throughout.
review: October 18, 1972 at St. Louis’ Fox Theatre captures the Grateful Dead in top form. The first set rolls through classics with a bright rendition of Bird Song and an uplifting China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider, while the second set erupts into a near‑70‑minute jam marathon anchored by Playin’ In The Band that launches into a powerful Dark Star → Morning Dew suite before looping back into Playin’. Highlights include a sweet‑sounding Jerry Garcia on lead and vocals, Phil Lesh’s colossal bass solo (the first Philo Stomp of the night), and Keith Godchaux’s clear, confident keyboards. Bob Weir adds a pleasant bite with his slightly distorted rhythm guitar, especially on Bertha and Big Railroad Blues, while Bill Kreutzmann drives the intensity with ferocious drumming and a crisp drum solo feeding into Dark Star. The soundboard capture is clean and balanced, making this show a must‑listen for anyone chasing that 1972 fire.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All five recordings are uniformly positive, repeatedly praising the Playin'→Dark Star→Morning Dew suite, Bird Song, and strong individual performances (Phil’s bass solo, Jerry’s lead work). Consistent acclaim across multiple sources justifies a high positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Solid soundboard with clear mix, minor hiss only; captures the high‑energy suite and band interplay exceptionally well.

key_highlights: Iconic Playin’ → Dark Star → Morning Dew marathon (≈70 min) with seamless segues
Bird Song delivers sweet vocal harmonies and soaring guitar wails
China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider shines as a bright first‑set transition
Bertha features unusually audible Bob Weir fills in the remastered mix

band_performance.Jerry: Lead guitar is on fire with memorable wails on Bird Song and Morning Dew; vocals are sweet and sit well in the mix despite occasional low levels.
band_performance.Phil: Telepathic, colossal bass work highlighted by a standout Philo Stomp and dominant solo during Dark Star and Morning Dew.
band_performance.Bob: Gritty rhythm guitar with pleasant distortion adds bite; audible lead fills on Bertha and Big Railroad Blues make his contribution stand out.
band_performance.Keith: Clear, present keyboards that stay rooted throughout; delivers some of his best solos and shines especially in the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Billy: Ferocious, hard‑driving drumming with a crisp drum solo that propels the Dark Star transition.
